patch.value: describeExtensionBlueprint omits importer/exporter-with-options command definitions (parity gap vs describeExtensionActivation)

describeExtensionBlueprint (src/sdk/compose.ts) places importer/exporter synthesized command paths only in command_handlers, never in `commands`, and does not surface their option-registered flag targets or the resulting `commands` capability. But at runtime, registerImporter/registerExporter WITH options (action/description/flags) push a full RegisteredExtensionCommandDefinition (loader applyImportExportCommandMetadata), so describeExtensionActivation lists those paths under `commands`, includes their `<name> import|export` flag targets in flag_commands, and reports `commands` in capabilities. For an importer/exporter-with-options-only blueprint the two summaries diverge (commands, flag_commands, capabilities), violating the parity contract pinned in the describeExtensionBlueprint docstring. The existing parity test (buildParityBlueprint) masks this because its importers/exporters declare no options. Empirically confirmed while building pm-lfdv: an importers-only blueprint with options.flags yields static commands=[] / capabilities=[importers,schema] vs runtime commands=[tickets export,tickets import] / capabilities=[commands,importers,schema]. Fix: make describeExtensionBlueprint mirror applyImportExportCommandMetadata for entries with options (commands + flag_commands + command capability), then extend the parity test with a flag-bearing importer/exporter. Related: pm-v3ty (deriveExtensionCapabilities schema under-grant, fixed alongside pm-lfdv).
